text: I created this portfolio to introduce myself and share my professional background, learning journey, projects, and interests in a way that allows more context than a traditional CV or social media profile.

I began my career as a chemistry examiner at the Estonian Patent Office, where I analyzed patent applications and worked with scientific literature and technical documentation. Later I moved into roles in sales and customer relations, where communication, organization, and practical problem-solving became central parts of my work.

Over the past few years I have also been exploring software development, building websites and small applications as a way to learn and create useful things. Technology has become an exciting field for me, both intellectually and creatively.

At the same time, my experience and interests are broader than a single role. I enjoy working with people, organizing projects, solving practical problems, and helping ideas turn into reality — whether that happens in IT, business or another collaborative environment.
Here I share some of the experiences, projects, and interests that have shaped my path.

items.title: Sales Manager, Hoberg & Driesch Finland Oy
text: Built and developed the company’s B2B client base in the Estonian market through outbound sales and business development.
Managed the full sales cycle, including pricing, negotiations, and long-term client relationship management.
Organized and conducted client meetings while developing and maintaining business partnerships.
Monitored orders and transaction progress, proactively identifying risks and ensuring a smooth delivery process.

items.title: Account Assistant, ArcelorMittal Commercial Baltics OÜ (ArcelorMittal – one of the world’s largest steel producers)
text: Managed customer orders throughout the entire lifecycle, from production planning to final delivery, ensuring smooth information flow between all parties involved.
Coordinated daily communication between clients, production, and logistics teams to ensure deadlines were met and the supply chain operated efficiently.
Prepared price quotations and internal reports, supporting the sales process and data-driven decision making.
Resolved customer claims and operational issues, identifying practical solutions while maintaining strong client relationships.

items.title: Chemistry, Medicine and Foodstuffs Examiner, Estonian Patent Office
text: Assessed patent applications for compliance with legal requirements and evaluated the quality and completeness of the submitted documentation.
Conducted comprehensive patent searches in international databases to evaluate the novelty and inventive step of inventions.
Analyzed technical and scientific information and prepared official decisions and correspondence based on the findings.
Advised patent applicants and explained the patent examination process and related requirements.

items.title: MyStuffLabelled — Personal Inventory with QR Labels
description: Originally a small personal project to explore backend development and QR-based labeling, the application now evolved into a collaborative team project under the TalTech “From Junior to Senior Software Developer program”.
The goal is to build a practical tool for organizing personal belongings, allowing users to register items, generate labels, and access item details by scanning them.
The project is being developed as both a web and mobile application, with a planned public release in May 2026.
highlights: Secure login and item ownership logic
QR code generation for physical labeling
REST API communication between backend and Vue frontend
Ongoing development: admin dashboard, search and filtering, item sharing
items.techStack: Tech stack: Java (Spring Boot), PostgreSQL, Vue.js, REST API, MapStruct

text: In 2014 I discovered swing dancing — a hobby that quickly became much more than just a pastime. Besides the joy of dancing itself, I was fascinated by the welcoming and diverse community where people from very different backgrounds could connect through music and movement.

This hobby has taken me to many cities and countries and has given me a new perspective on culture, community, and collaboration. Over the years I have dedicated a lot of time to learning different swing dance styles and supporting the growth of our local scene.

Since 2017 I have been teaching regular dance classes and became part of the Tallinn Swing Dance Society (NGO) team. Together with this enthusiastic group we have grown the local swing dance community and organized workshops, social dance events, local parties, and international festivals.
Even after more than a decade in the scene, I still feel that I continue to grow — not only as a dancer and instructor, but also as an organizer and as a person.
